posts.hook: Blue light glasses: worth it, or a $50 placebo?
posts.caption:
The science on blue light glasses:

✓ Block some high-energy blue light
✗ No clinical evidence they reduce eye strain
✗ Placebo effect in most studies

What actually reduces digital eye strain:
→ The 20-20-20 rule (done properly)
→ Proper screen distance (arm's length)
→ Structured exercise breaks
→ Lubricating eye drops for dry eyes

Save your $50.

Free evidence-based tools: axeo.vision

#BlueLightGlasses #EyeStrain #EyeHealth #Axeo #AxeoVision #DigitalEyeStrain #DebunkingMyths
posts.visual_notes: Side-by-side: blue light glasses vs actual solutions. Fact-check style with sources. Scientific but accessible. Credibility-first aesthetic.
posts.video_prompt: Runway Gen-4: Split-screen comparison on dark background. NO PEOPLE. LEFT (5s): a pair of blue-light glasses resting on a desk, cool blue screen glow behind them. White label fades in: 'BLUE LIGHT GLASSES'. Then three red ✗ bullet points appear below: 'No clinical evidence', 'AAO doesn't recommend', 'Placebo in most studies'. RIGHT (5s): clean teal checklist on dark background. Label: 'WHAT ACTUALLY WORKS'. Three teal ✓ points appear: '20-20-20 rule', 'Arm's length distance', 'Structured breaks'. Thin vertical white divider between panels. Crisp, medical-credibility aesthetic. 9:16. 14 seconds.

posts.hook: Most people have never done a proper Snellen test. Here's how it works.
posts.caption:
The Snellen chart isn't just a random letter grid.

Here's the science:

- Each row is calibrated for a specific distance
- 20/20 means you see at 20 feet what a 'normal' eye sees at 20 feet
- 20/40 means your eye needs 20 feet to see what others see at 40

Most people haven't had their vision tested since school.

Full Snellen test — free, instant, no sign-up:
axeo.vision/test

#SnellenTest #VisionTest #Axeo #AxeoVision #EyeExam #20x20 #VisionCare
posts.visual_notes: Animated Snellen chart explanation. Show distance annotations, normal range markers. Transition to Axeo test interface. Educational, clean, medical credibility.
posts.video_prompt: Runway Gen-4: Two-part sequence. NO PEOPLE. PART 1 (10s): A Snellen eye chart fills the frame, softly lit on a white clinical wall. Chart begins blurry (simulated myopic blur — heavy gaussian, soft edges), then slowly sharpens to crisp clarity over 6 seconds as if optically corrected. Subtle lens flare as sharpness peaks. Hold sharp for 2s. PART 2 (8s): animated infographic overlay on dark background — the same chart with two rows highlighted: top row annotated '20 ft / person sees this' in teal, lower row '40 ft / normal eye sees this' in white. A teal arrow shows the 20-foot viewing distance. Clean, medical-grade typography. End with 'Take your free test' fade-in. 9:16. 25 seconds.

posts.hook: I took the Snellen test on my phone. Here's my score — and what it means.
posts.caption:
20/20 vision doesn't mean perfect eyes.

It means your eyesight matches the standard.

My score: 20/25 — slightly below standard, common for heavy screen users.

Take your own free test: axeo.vision/test

posts.hook: I spent $200 researching whether blue light glasses work. Here's what I found.
posts.caption:
Spent way too much researching blue light glasses.

Key findings:
- Blue light IS real and emitted by screens
- Evidence for harm to eyes: inconclusive
- American Academy of Ophthalmology: doesn't recommend blue light glasses for eye strain
- Placebo studies show similar improvement with clear lenses

What the AAO DOES recommend: 20-20-20, proper lighting, regular breaks

All of which are free.

axeo.vision/exercise

#BlueLightScience #EyeHealth #Axeo #AAO #EyeStrain #HealthTech
posts.visual_notes: Research breakdown format. Show AAO citation. Balanced, credible. Not dismissive — just accurate.

posts.hook: Blue light glasses: the AAO's position vs. what the marketing says.
posts.caption:
The AAO (American Academy of Ophthalmology) does not recommend blue light glasses for eye strain.

Their recommended interventions:
→ 20-20-20 rule
→ Proper screen distance
→ Adequate room lighting
→ Regular exercise breaks

All free. All evidence-based.

axeo.vision/exercise
